articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 46
articles.content_text: 60% 3 Li Lithium 6.941 The emergence of various wireless electronic products such as laptops, tablet PCs, smartphones, wireless earphones, and even cordless vacuum and portable televisions has not only made our life more convenient but they have now increasingly become essential parts of our lives. The key player enabling this “transition to a cordless era” is the lithium-ion battery, and lithium, its core material, has gained even greater value with the advent of the electric vehicle era. Lithium, now as precious as “white gold”, was discovered in 1817 by the Swedish chemist Johan August Arfwedson 17921841 While chemically analyzing petalite a type of mineral , he found an element that f zzes and explodes when placed in water. It contained about 4% of an unknown alkali metal. The new element was named after the Greek word lithos, meaning “stone, rock”, because it was found in a mineral as opposed to other alkali metals like sodium and potassium, which had been discovered in plants. Although Arfwedson failed to isolate the element due to the absence of an electrolysis machine at the time, British chemist Humphry Davy 17781829 , succeeded in extracting pure lithium in 1818. Lithium’s atomic number is 3, and the symbol is “Li”. An alkali metal that belongs to the Group 1 of the periodic table, its atomic mass is 6.941 g/㏖, while its melting and boiling points are 180.54 and 1,347°C, respectively, and its density is 0.534 g/㎤. This silvery-white metal is the lightest among all metals and it has the lowest density of all solid elements, meaning and it is soft enough to be cut with a knife. Lithium is found only in compounds in nature, and as it reacts violently with water, it is normally stored in oil such as paraff n wax. Although lithium is being used for many purposes, its most prominent industrial application currently is in batteries. It is used for the cathode among the four major elements of lithium-ion batteries composed of cathode, anode, electrolyte, and separator. Lithium offers high voltage and has the advantage of extra high energy density as well as fast charging and discharging. Because of these highly desirable features, lithium has become the mainstream in the battery field. Major lithiumproducing countries include Australia, China, the United States of America, Chile, Brazil, Argentina, and Bolivia, with the Latin American countries holding 60% of the world’s lithium reserves, making it a pivotal area. Cathode Material ➀ The Core of Lithium-Ion Batteries, Lithium Part 3. Forming the Core of Batteries ㅣ Materials of Secondary Batteries46

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 47
articles.content_text: Co Cobalt 58.933 27 Cobalt, used as a blue colorant since the Bronze Age, plays a significant role in the modern battery industry. The name cobalt originated from “kobold”, a term German miners used to call the ores containing cobalt. The existence of this cobalt was revealed around 1735 when the Swedish chemist Georg Brandt 16941768 first isolated cobalt from cobalt ore. However, chemists at the time assumed it to be a compound of iron and arsenic or copper. This was because both cobalt and copper compounds both exhibited blue color. In 1780, Swedish chemist Torbern Olof Bergman 17351784 reaffrmed that cobalt was a new metal, and it was fnally recognized as an element on its own. Cobalt’s atomic number is 27, and its element symbol is “Co”. It is an iron group element in Group 9 of the periodic table that is hard and ferromagnetic retaining magnetism even when an external magnetic feld is removed This silvery-white metallic element is stable in air and does not react with water, but slowly dissolves in dilute acids. Naturally, it is found combined with arsenic or sulfur, and it is mainly found in smaltite but may also be present in copper and lead ores. Until the early 20th century, cobalt had few uses other than coloring pigment. However, it began to capture attention when an American businessman named Elwood Haynes introduced “Stellite” alloy, a superalloy maintaining strength and shape at high temperatures. Currently, cobalt is garnering attention again as it is a core material of lithium-ion batteries, the backbone of the electric vehicle industry. This is because cobalt is essential in controlling corrosion or explosion hazards of the cathode material, which critically determines the performance of high energy density electric vehicle batteries. Primarily produced as a byproduct of copper and nickel mining, cobalt is a highly scarce element. Approximately 70% of the global supply originates in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This geographically disproportionate presence and a tight supply of cobalt often drive up its price, which accounts for over 20% of the battery manufacturing cost. On the other hand, cobalt has emerged as a global issue due to problems arising from its mining and production, including environmental pollution. This has highlighted the importance of “responsible management of the cobalt supply chain” worldwide. Recognizing the need for ethical and transparent supply chain operations in battery production, LG Energy Solution, before its spin-off from LG Chem in 2019, has joined the “Responsible Minerals Initiative RMI ” and is working on establishing a proper and stable supply chain for cobalt and other raw battery materials. Cathode Material ➁ Essential Material for Cathode Production, Cobalt ENSOLPEDIA47

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 48
articles.content_text: Axel Fredrik Cronstedt 28 Ni Nickel 58.693 Nickel, traditionally used in coinage, has an interesting discovery story. In medieval Germany, miners found red ores that looked like copper ore in Erzgebirge area. This ore mainly consisted of nickel arsenide NiAs which is called niccolite now. However, the miners believed the ores contained copper and attempted to extract it, only to release poisonous gas arsenic trioxide, As2O3 instead of copper. This led them to call the ore “Kupfernickel,” meaning “devils copper”. Then in 1751, Swedish metallurgist Axel Fredrik Cronstedt 17221765 also made efforts to isolate copper. While conducting a reduction experiment with the oxides from the surface of Kupfernickel, he obtained a white metal instead of copper. This turned out to be a new element, which he named “nickel”, dropping the “kupfer meaning copper ” part of the name. Nickel’s atomic number is 28, and its symbol is “Ni”. Its atomic weight is 58. 693 g/㏖, while its melting and boiling points are 1,455 and 2,732°C, respectively, and its density is 8.9 g/㎤. Hard, tough, and markedly resistant to oxidation in the air, this silvery-white metal is widely used for plating or as a material for making an alloy. Nickel, previously used mostly for manufacturing stainless steel, is now playing a vital role in electric vehicle batteries as a key element in cathode materials. Cathode materials are made by combining nickel, cobalt, manganese, and aluminum, and their composition determines the batterys capacity and performance. The higher the nickel content is, the higher the energy density is, which increases the driving range per charge. Therefore, the battery industry is working to reduce the amount of expensive cobalt in cathode materials while increasing the proportion of nickel, which enhances energy and power. Batteries that have the cathode with a high nickel content are called “High-Nickel High-Ni batteries”, and the industry predicts that these will be the most preferred kind for electric vehicle batteries. As demand for nickel grows, securing nickel is also becoming important. It is reported that the largest holder of nickel reserves is Indonesia, accounting for about 30% of the global production. Hence, LG Energy Solution has gained nickel mining rights in Indonesia while making an agreement with its government to build a battery cell plant of 10 GWh gigawatt-hours a year in a joint investment with Hyundai Motor Group. By doing so, LG Energy Solution expects to produce batteries cooperatively as well as secure nickel in the country. Cathode Material ➂ Increasing Energy Density with Nickel Part 3. Forming the Core of Batteries ㅣ Materials of Secondary Batteries48

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 49
articles.content_text: 25 Mn Manganese 54.938 Manganese, commonly encountered as a dietary supplement, also plays an important role in enhancing the battery stability. This element, which we often call “Mangan” following the German notation, is referred to as “Manganese” in English, according to the naming convention set by the IUPAC International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry Manganese’s atomic number is 25, and its symbol is “Mn”. It is a brittle, silvery-white metal that belongs to Group 7 of the periodic table with an atomic weight of 54.938 g/㏖, a melting point of 1,247°C, and a density of 7.2 g/㎤. Notable for its high reactivity, manganese oxidizes slowly when in the form of mass in the air while it ignites when in powder form. Also, it produces hydrogen gas in contact with water. Manganese, the twelfth most abundant metal in Earths crust, is widely distributed in the form of pyrolusite manganese dioxide, MnO2 In 1774, Swedish chemist Carl Wilhelm Scheele 17421786 discovered that pyrolusite contained a new element, and his colleague Johan Gottlieb Gahn 17451818 successfully isolated an impure sample of manganese metal. High-purity 99.9% manganese was obtained about 160 years later in the 1930s through the electrolysis of Mn²+ solution. In the early 1800s, manganese emerged as an alloying element that imparts strength to iron. In 1866, the Leclanche cell that uses MnO2 as the cathode material was invented. Then in 1912, manganese phosphating, a type of coating with special benefits of increased corrosion resistance and wear protection when applied to steel, was invented and has been widely adopted in manufacturing car parts. With the advent of the electric vehicle era, manganese has gained renewed attention. The growth in demand for electric vehicles gave rise to demand for medium- and large-sized secondary batteries, and the most common cathode material for those batteries is NCM nickel, cobalt, and manganese Recently, NCMA nickel, cobalt, manganese, aluminum , a safer cathode material that has a higher portion of nickel and contains aluminum, was developed. As such, energy density has been raised with additional nickel, and stability has been enhanced with aluminum. In these cathode materials, manganese plays a pivotal role in stabilizing the cathode structure and is therefore used as an essential material in battery manufacturing. Cathode Material ➃ Manganese Contributing to Battery Stability ENSOLPEDIA49

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 50
articles.content_text: Ternary Cathode Battery NCA Cathode current collector 13 AI Aluminium 26.982 Aluminum is a very familiar material to us and we commonly encounter aluminum in everyday life as aluminum foil, often referred to as “tin foil”, and in beverage cans. In the mobility era, now, however, aluminum should be remembered as a key material in lithium-ion batteries. Aluminum’s atomic number is 13, and its symbol is “Al”. Pure aluminum forms a thin oxide layer when exposed to air, which, despite losing its luster, prevents internal corrosion. It is the third most abundant element in Earth’s crust, after oxygen and silicon, and the most common metallic element. It once existed only in the form of oxides because of its high oxygen affnity. The metal can be obtained from bauxite that is composed primarily of aluminum hydroxide. However, in the past, aluminum purifcation was so costly that the metal was called “the silver from clay”. The price of aluminum stabilized after the development of the “Hall–Héroult process”, an “electrolytic smelting method”, in 1886. This refning technique, almost simultaneously discovered by American chemist Charles Martin Hall and French chemist Paul Louis-Toussaint Héroult, has led to its widespread use as a raw material in various industries today. Since aluminum is light with high ductility and malleability, it can easily be shaped into wires or foils. Its high thermal and electrical conductivity makes it suitable for high-tension cables or kitchen utensils. Additionally, it is used for beverage cans, airplanes, ships, and vehicles due to its lightweight, durability, and corrosion resistance. Aluminum is also a metal with a high recycling rate. Since recycling aluminum demands less energy and is more effcient than producing it, many countries encourage recycling the metal. Currently, aluminum is an indispensable material in the manufacture of lithium-ion batteries. This is because it contributes to the improvement of battery power as a cathode material in ternary batteries NCA and quaternary batteries NCMA. Moreover, it is used as an important cathode current collector in the battery electrode manufacturing process. During the coating process, where binders are evenly dispersed onto the electrode, the cathode slurry is thinly coated onto aluminum foil. Therefore, as the demand for high-performing, lightweight batteries increases, the use of aluminum is expected to grow as well. Cathode Material ➄ Aluminum in Enhancing Battery Power Part 3. Forming the Core of Batteries ㅣ Materials of Secondary Batteries50

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 51
articles.content_text: LiOH Lithium carbonate Lithium hydroxide Lithium hydroxide is gaining prominence with the growth of the electric vehicle market. Because of its significant role in realizing high-energy batteries. Lithium, a key material of lithium-ion batteries, comes in various forms such as lithium carbonate and lithium hydroxide that are used in manufacturing lithium-ion batteries. These materials have different applicationslithium carbonate is mainly used in batteries for small electronic devices such as laptops and IT devices, while lithium hydroxide is primarily employed for high-density, high-energy batteries such as those used in electric vehicle batteries, and lithium hydroxide is receiving attention accordingly. Lithium hydroxide has the chemical formula “LiOH”, and is a white solid that is a strong base, highly soluble in water, and absorbs carbon dioxide from the air. It is obtained largely through two methodsExtracting from brine or using physical and chemical processes on lithium ores. The reason lithium hydroxide is suitable for manufacturing lithium-ion batteries is due to its easy synthesis with nickel, a key ingredient in battery cathode materials. The higher the nickel content in a cathode, the more the battery energy density is. Recently, the popularity of high-nickel content batteries has pushed up the demand for lithium hydroxide. As a response, LG Energy Solution has signed a long-term offtake agreement with Germany’s Vulcan Energy Resources Ltd., a company that produces eco-friendly lithium, for a stable supply of environmentally sustainable lithium hydroxide. Vulcan Energy extracts lithium using eco-friendly geothermal energy in the Upper Rhine Valley in Germany. LG Energy Solution is expected to receive a total of 45,000 tons of lithium hydroxide over f ve years starting from 2025. Cathode Material ➅ Key Material for High-Energy BatteriesLithium Hydroxide Used in batteries for small electronic devices such as laptops and IT products Used in high-density, high-capacity batteries for electric vehicles, etc. ENSOLPEDIA51

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 52
articles.content_text: Mining at lithium mines Extracting from salt lakes Check Among the cathode materials for electric vehicle batteries, lithium is receiving special attention. When encountering related news, issues related to its price surge come across a lot, indicating the inseparable relationship between secondary batteries and lithium. Why is lithium important for secondary batteries? Since lithium is a key cathode material that determines the capacity and voltage of a lithium-ion battery, it receives signif cant news coverage, especially when its price undergoes major changes. The cathode material is made by mixing lithium with precursors composed of materials like nickel, cobalt, and manganese. However, since lithium is hard to utilize in its pure form, it is used after processing into a compound. The types of lithium compounds used in battery manufacturing include lithium hydroxide LiOH and lithium carbonate Li 2CO3 Lithium hydroxide and lithium carbonate differ in their applications. Lithium hydroxide is mainly used for electric vehicle batteries that feature high energy density and high capacity. This is because it is easier to synthesize especially high nickel-containing cathodes for high energy density lithium-ion batteries. These batteries made using lithium hydroxide are generally classif ed as nickel-based ternary cathode batteries, such as NCM and NCA. A ternary cathode battery is a battery with three metal elements of lithium cobalt oxide LCO , nickel, and another in its cathode. Recently, demand for lithium hydroxide is rising faster as high nickel batteries that have a nickel content of more than 80% are preferred due to higher energy density. Lithium carbonate is mainly used to make LFP Lithium Iron Phosphate batteries for electric vehicles with iron phosphate in the cathode, as well as batteries for home electronics and IT devices that demand relatively low energy density. An LFP battery is a battery with a cathode material consisting of lithium iron phosphate LFP , a combination where iron phosphate replaces cobalt of lithium cobalt oxide LCO Difference between Lithium Compounds Lithium Hydroxide VS Lithium Carbonate Difference between Lithium Hydroxide and Lithium Carbonate52

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 53
articles.content_text: Commercial lithium hydroxide or carbonate can be obtained by either mining at lithium mines or extracting from salt lakes a lake that has a high concentration of salts Once pure lithium is obtained, it goes through the process of converting into compounds for appropriate usage. Mined lithium ores can be turned into lithium hydroxide or lithium carbonate through further processing like heating and f ltration. Also, lithium salts acquired from salt lakes can be transformed into lithium carbonate by evaporating the salt water, and then into lithium hydroxide by processing or converting the lithium carbonate. Lithium is expected to be used as a core material not only in the currently popular lithium-ion batteries but also in next-generation batteries such as all-solid-state and lithium-sulfur batteries. Thus, the demand for lithium is expected to continue growing. According to the United States Geological Survey USGS ’s 2021 report, global lithium reserves were 89 million tons. Main lithium producers include Australia, Chile, China, Argentina, and Brazil, with Latin American countries holding 60% of the total lithium reserves. However, it is diff cult to immediately respond to the demands since mining lithium takes a long time, and additional processing is necessary to produce lithium compounds. Lithium requires considerable time to transition from mining to lithium compounds, making it challenging to meet demand promptly. Lithium accounts for 6070% of cathode material costs, and cathode materials account for 52% of battery costs. Since the cost increase of lithium leads to the price increase of batteries, and consequently, of electric vehicles, it is crucial to secure lithium in advance. Thus, as mentioned earlier, LG Energy Solution is diversifying its supply chain for lithium carbonate and lithium hydroxide to respond to the current trends in the global raw material market by signing supply agreement for lithium hydroxide with companies such as Vulcan Energy of Germany and will be supplied 45,000 tons of lithium hydroxide for f ve years from 2025. In addition, in November 2022, LG Energy Solution signed a long-term supply contract for lithium carbonate with Compass Minerals and will be supplied 40% of the annual lithium carbonate produced by the American company for seven years following 2025. The supply contracts are even more meaningful as both Vulcan Energy and Compass Minerals produce lithium in an eco-friendly way. Such achievements will help LG Energy Solution strengthen its ESG Environmental, Social, Governance competitiveness for carbon neutrality. Lithium Hydroxide and Lithium Carbonate Production Process Efforts to Secure Lithium Lithium Price Forecast 2035 SourceSNE Research 2025 , 2026 Lithium Metal Report 2035 [RMB/kg] SourceSNE Research 2025 , 2026 Lithium Metal Report 2035 [kt, LCE Lithium Carbonate Equivalent ] Global Battery Lithium Demand Forecast by Cathode 600 500 400 300 200 100 0 2021 2023 2025 2027 2030 2035 Lithium Carbonate 2024 1,075 CAGR 9.7% 2,976 2025 2026 2028 2030 2032 2035 ENSOLPEDIA53

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 54
articles.content_text: 16 S Sulfur 32.065 Lithium-sulfur batteries are emerging as the next-generation battery after lithium-ion batteries. This is because it is cheaper, lighter, and more environmentally friendly. Sulfur is the key material required to manufacture this battery. Sulfur appears in the Old Testament account, where it is mentioned in stories about the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah cities of sin and corruption with f re and sulfur. The English term for sulfur also originates from the Latin “sulphrium”, derived from the Sanskrit word “sulvere”, meaning “origin of f re”. Sulfur’s atomic number is 16, and its symbol is “S”. It is a nonmetallic element that is the 16th most abundant element on the Earth, and is mostly found in volcanic regions. Sulfur is a yellow solid at room temperature, but when ignited, it burns with a blue f ame, emitting sulfur dioxide SO2 that has an unpleasant odor. Sulfur has been used since ancient times. The Egyptians used it in pharmaceutical applications, for bleaching fabrics, or disinfecting their homes with the gas produced by burning it. Ancient alchemists regarded sulfur as a crucial substance so much that they believed that “all substances of the world are made of a mixture of sulfur and mercury”. Sulfur was recognized as an element, not a compound, only in 1777 by French chemist Antoine Laurent de Lavoisier 17431794 Since then, sulfur has been widely used in various industries for making matches, pharmaceuticals, fertilizers, pesticides, paper, textiles, and metals. The amount of sulfur consumption is now an indicator of the economic level of a nation. It is present in human hair, nails, and skin. It is also in amino acids that are included in garlic. The smell of burning hair and the pungent f avor of garlic are attributable to sulfur. Sulfur’s importance in the battery industry arises from lithium-sulfur batteries being identif ed as the next-generation batteries as mentioned above. These batteries use sulfur as the cathode material and lithium as the anode material, enabling the production of lightweight, high-capacity, and high energy density batteries. Also, it has price competitiveness since the abundant sulfur replaces rare metals such as cobalt and nickel. Thus, if commercialized, lithium batteries are expected to be in high demand to be actively used for use in future transportation, such as drones and aircraft. Cathode Material ➆ The Key Material for Next-Generation Batteries, Sulfur Part 3. Forming the Core of Batteries ㅣ Materials of Secondary Batteries54

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 55
articles.content_text: Mg2+ Mg2+ 12 Mg Magnesium 24.305 Magnesium is an abundant element that can be obtained from seawater, as well as the Earth’s crust. Its existence was first introduced about 400 years ago. In Epsom, Surrey, England, people found bitter-tasting water, which contained magnesium sulfate. Later, British chemist Humphry Davy 17781829 frst isolated the metal through electrolysis. Magnesium’s atomic number is 12, and its symbol is “Mg”. Magnesium is a silvery-white metal at room temperature with its density of 1.738 g/㎤, which is softer than aluminum. This 8th most abundant element in Earth’s crust is found in minerals such as magnesite and dolomite. In the seawater, it is found in the form of magnesium ions and is the third most common element after chloride and sodium ions. In the industrial sector, magnesium is the third most used metal after iron and aluminum. Magnesium’s low density and high reaction make it a good alloying agent. In particular, magnesiumaluminum alloys are lightweight and extensively used in the aerospace industries. In addition, it is also essential for human health, contributing to controlling energy metabolism, body temperature, and muscle contraction, as well as keeping bones and teeth strong. Recently, magnesium has been considered in the battery industry as a potential substitute for expensive lithium. Magnesium shares similar chemical properties with lithium while being more abundant and easier to recycle, thus leading to inexpensive and more sustainable battery production. Cathode Material ➇ Emerging as an Alternative to Lithium, Magnesium ENSOLPEDIA55

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 56
articles.content_text: The structure of graphite Layers of carbon in a plate-like form stacked on top of each other 6 C Carbon 12.0107 Graphite is a familiar material to us, commonly found in pencils and mechanical pencil leads. Besides these uses, graphite plays a crucial role hidden from view as a material in lithium-ion batteries. Graphite consists solely of carbon and is soft with a hardness of 1.5 out of 10, and a density of about 2.23 g/㎤. The glossy black or iron-gray mineral is the perfect material for pencil, as its name suggests; the word “graphite” comes from the Greek verb “graphein”, “to write”. In graphite, each carbon atom is bonded with three other structures, forming a thin plane. Although both graphite and diamond are composed of pure carbon, they have different crystal structures. Diamond has a close-knit, dense structure in which each carbon atom is joined to four other carbon atoms which results in hardness and durability. The bond between the planes is easily breakable, but the layer structure makes graphite a good choice as the anode of a lithium-ion battery. The role of the anode of a lithium-ion battery is storing and releasing lithium ions arriving from the cathode + , which the graphite’s layer structures enable. However, graphite expands when it stores lithium ions, increasing slightly in volume. As the battery is repeatedly charged and discharged, the structure of graphite gradually changes, undermining the battery’s capability to store lithium ions and eventually reducing battery life. Ultimately, a lithium-ion battery’s lifespan is determined by the anode material, which normally has a shorter life than that of the cathode material. To improve, synthetic graphite is sometimes used instead of natural graphite. Synthetic graphite is manufactured by applying heat above 2,500°C, a treatment for producing a uniform and stable internal structure than natural graphite. Therefore, it undergoes less structural change with repetitive charging and discharging, thereby extending battery life. Also, its structure offers more paths for lithium-ion transportation, making it advantageous for rapid charging compared to natural graphite. Anode Material ➀ The Leading Anode Material for Storing Lithium Ions, Graphite Part 3. Forming the Core of Batteries ㅣ Materials of Secondary Batteries56

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 57
articles.content_text: Silicon 14 Si Silicon 28.085 Even if the cathode material produces a lot of energy, it cannot be efficiently utilized if the anode material does not function properly. This is because the anode material stores and releases lithium ions from the cathode + , generating electricity. Graphite has long been used as an anode material, but with the increasing performance and capacity demands on batteries, there is a growing need for next-generation anode materials capable of higher capacity and faster charging. Silicon Si is emerging as the material of choice to replace graphite in this regard. While graphite stores one lithium ion per six carbon atoms, silicon can store up to 4.4 lithium ions per atom. In other words, silicon anode materials have four times more capacity per gram than graphite anode materials. Consequently, using silicon in anode materials can dramatically improve energy density, extending the driving range of electric vehicles and making rapid charging of batteries more feasible. At the same time, silicon is economical and eco-friendly, which makes it a more attractive candidate as the next-generation anode material. Silicon’s atomic number is 14, and its symbol is “Si”. A metalloid, silicon constitutes about 27.6% of the Earth’s crust by weight and is the second most abundant element after oxygen. It is found as silica or silicate in nature and is one of the major components of sand and earth. Research on silicon began in 1787 by French chemist Antoine Laurent de Lavoisier 17431794 , but the technology of the time couldn’t extract the element in pure form, so silica, an oxide of silicon, was classified as an element. Later in 1824, Swedish chemist Jöns Jacob Berzelius 17791848 succeeded in reducing silicon to its elemental form. Silicon has been a key material in the semiconductor industry since the late 20th century and is now poised to lead the anode material market in the battery industry. Experts anticipate signifcant growth in the silicon anode material market, projecting that its value will rise from approximately $600 million in 2023 to $6.6 billion by 2035. Of course, there are still issues to be resolved with silicon anode materials, such as the volume expansion that takes place during lithiation and the tendency to fracture. Various research efforts are underway to address these issues, including using graphite or graphene in combination with silicon. Nevertheless, LG Energy Solution, through technological innovation, applied an anode material containing 5% silicon for the first time in battery electric vehicles in 2019 and is currently developing “Pure Silicon” technology, consisting of 100% silicon. Anode Material ➁ Accelerating the Charging Speed of Electric Vehicles, Silicon ENSOLPEDIA57

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 58
articles.content_text: Cu Copper 63.546 29 Copper has been a part of human history for a long time through its use in coins. The word for “coin” in Korean uses the Chinese character “dong 銅 ”, which means copper. Although the use of coins is increasingly declining, leading to a reduced awareness of copper, it is expected to remain an important element for humans as it is used as a key component in battery anode materials. Humans f rst used copper around 8,000 B.C.E. It has been widely used for a long time since it is found in a directly usable metallic form in nature. The introduction of copper contributed to the development of quenching and smelting techniques, the birth of bronze, a copper-tin alloy, and the establishment of the iron-smelting technique. As such, it has made a notable impact on human history. Copper’s atomic number is 29, and its symbol is “Cu”. It is approximately the 25th most abundant element in Earth’s crust and one of the most recycled metals after steel and aluminum. In Roman times, copper was mined from the island of Cyprus, hence it was called “cyprium” meaning “a metal of Cyprus”. Later, it was referred to as “cuprum” in Latin, which evolved into “copper” in English. Copper is a soft metal that is easily bent, and stretched thin. It is a good conductor of heat and electricity that gets hardened when alloyed with other metals. Such properties make copper a good ingredient for diverse products across the industry from items that require high thermal and electrical conductivity such as electric wires, kitchen utensils, electronic devices, heat pipes, and electric vehicles to ship propellers, wind instruments, door knobs, and valves. Another property of copper is that it oxidizes to form a green rust that is called verdigris or patina on the surface. Since the verdigris layer protects the underlying copper from further corrosion, it is often used for roofs of buildings. In the battery industry, copper is indispensable for the fabrication of the anode. The copper foil has on it the coating of the anode material of a battery and takes the role of a current collector that collects and supplies electrons generated during electrochemical reactions. The current collector has to be as thin as possible for lightweight of the battery. Accordingly, a ductile, highly conductive copper foil was adopted to produce the anode current collector, for batteries. With the increasing use of electric vehicles and electronic devices, copper is expected to play an even more signif cant role in the future. Anode Material ➂ Contributing to Battery Lightweighting and High Capacity, Copper Part 3. Forming the Core of Batteries ㅣ Materials of Secondary Batteries58

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 59
articles.content_text: Crystal structures of ceramics CINa+ One of the oldest materials used by humanity is ceramic. The origins of ceramic products can be traced back to prehistoric times when people f red clay to create pottery and ceramics. The word “ceramic” originates from the ancient Greek word κεραμɩκός keramikos , meaning “an object made of clay and f red”. Ceramics, familiar to us through everyday items such as pottery dishes, glass, and tiles, are recently being used as a key material in electric vehicle battery separators, expanding their applications. Ceramic is a non-metallic solid formed by combining metallic elements with non-metallic elements like oxygen, nitrogen, and carbon under heat. What matters, in particular, are the ionic bonds. The crystal structures of ceramics can vary depending on the amount of the charge of ions comprising the material as well as the sizes of cations metal and the anions non-metal These ceramics are hard and can withstand large temperature changes. They are also resistant to corrosion under strong acidic and basic conditions. “Fine ceramics”, improved through research, have advanced mechanical, chemical, electrical, and heat-resistant properties compared to traditional ceramics. Due to these advantages, f ne ceramics are used in advanced medical and scientific fields, including electronic parts, artificial teeth and bones, and insulators in the aerospace industry. In the battery industry, the role of ceramics is even more pronounced as ceramic particles and polymer binders improve battery durability and heat resistance further when coated onto the surface of the separator. This is the result of applying LG Energy Solution’s “Safety Reinforced Separator SRS® ” technology. Conventional separators lose durability at temperatures above 100°C; however, SRS® can withstand much higher temperatures. Because of this feature, safety can be enhanced even when it is applied to energy-dense and high-performance high-nickel batteries. Separator Material Enhancing Battery Durability, Ceramic ENSOLPEDIA59

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 60
articles.content_text: Insight Open, Transparent, and Ethical Operations LG Energy Solution’s Raw Material Supply Chain Management As nations worldwide strive to combat climate crises, such as declaring carbon neutrality, policies to reduce greenhouse gases have significantly expanded the electric vehicle market, leading to increased demand for battery raw materials. This demand has intensif ed competition among companies for raw material acquisition due to rising prices and potential supply shortages. Amidst this, LG Energy Solution is focusing on procuring raw materials expeditiously and responsibly. It is taking proactive steps like acquiring stakes in raw material companies, signing long-term supply contracts, and continuing activities in line with ESG management. Most importantly, as a leader in the global battery industry, it aims to operate a responsible supply chain that values human rights, ethics, and the environment. LG Energy Solution introduces its core tasks and activities to improve the sustainability of the battery raw material supply chain and manage it transparently and effectively. The Code of Conduct for Suppliers includes regulations and global standards that suppliers must follow to maintain a responsible supply chain, such as human rights and labor, ethical management, health and safety, environmental sustainability, responsible mineral purchasing, and grievance handling systems. LG Energy Solution communicates with its suppliers in accordance with these standards and will make every effort to reduce risks in the supply chain. Putting the Code of Conduct for Suppliers into Action Since an international human rights organization raised the issue of human rights labor in the raw material supply chain in 2016, LG Energy Solution has been conducting audits and establishing systemic management policies. It continuously verif es and improves supply chain risks for major raw materials such as cobalt, lithium, nickel, and natural graphite. In 2021, it independently joined the Responsible Minerals Initiative RMI to contribute to international efforts. Evaluation of Supply Chain Risks LG Energ y Solut ion operates a responsible sourcing system for key minerals, including nickel, cobalt, lithium, and natural graphite. In line with international standards such as the OECD Due Diligence Guidance, the company manages supply chain risks and continues to improve its practices by conducting due diligence and risk assessments on human rights, environmental, and ethical risks through its DDMS Due Diligence Management System Responsible Sourcing Policy Implementation LG Energy Solution identif es smelters within the cobalt supply chain based on CMRTs collected from its tier-1 suppliers. It aims to achieve 100% certif cation of identif ed smelters under the RMI’s RMAP Responsible Minerals Assurance Process by 2026, thereby strengthening supply chain transparency and reliability in line with global standards. Targeting RMAP Certif cation for Cobalt Smelters60

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 61
articles.content_text: LG Energy Solution has implemented a proactive risk identification process in its supply chain. To improve supply chain transparency, it introduced a blockchain platform that makes it impossible to manipulate or hack decentralized data storage, and it automatically syncs process-specific data to the platform to track and manage the history of raw material receipt and product production. It will gradually expand the raw material supply chain and partner companies that can use the platform in the future. Establishing Preventive Processes LG Energy Solution is implementing carbonreduction activities in its supply chain in order to achieve carbon neutrality across the value chain by 2050. It has identif ed hot spots in the supply chain that emit a lot of carbon by analyzing the carbon footprint data of the entire battery life cycle. It is also discussing detailed plans with its suppliers to convert 100% of the electricity used in raw material production to renewable energy. It meets with its suppliers regularly to convey the importance of carbon reduction, share regulatory trends, and investigate specific measures to assist them in switching to renewable energy under optimal conditions, considering the renewable energy procurement environment in each country. Carbon Reduction Activities in the Supply Chain LG Energy Solution’s Battery Raw Material Procurement History 2022 November Signed a supply contract with Compass Minerals USA for lithium carbonate and lithium hydroxide Scheduled to receive 40% of Compass Minerals’ annual production of lithium carbonate and lithium hydroxide for 7 years starting from 2025 2020 Signed a lithium supply contract with the world’s second-largest lithium producer, SQM of Chile Scheduled to receive 100,000 tons of lithium over 7 years starting from 2023 2019 First in Korea to join the “Responsible Minerals Initiative RMI ”, a coalition for responsible mineral procurement and supply chain management Practicing ESG in raw material procurement methods 2021 Invested approximately 35 billion KRW in China’s nickel and cobalt ref ning company GNCM Greatpower Nickel & Cobalt Minerals , acquiring a 4.8% stake and signing a long-term purchase agreement Scheduled to receive a total of 20,000 tons of nickel over 6 years starting from 2023 Invested approximately 12 billion KRW in Australia’s battery mineral ref ning specialist QPM Queensland Pacif c Metals , acquiring a 7.5% stake and signing a long-term purchase agreement Scheduled to receive annually 7,000 tons of ecofriendly produced nickel and 700 tons of cobalt for 10 years starting from 2025 ENSOLPEDIA61

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 64
articles.content_text: Batteries come in various forms, such as cylindrical, pouch-type, and prismatic, but the basic manufacturing method is very similar. They are manufactured by assembling cathode, anode, separators, and electrolytes. However, the shape in which they are assembled and packaged affects not only their appearance but also their energy density and production diffculty. When we think of batteries, the cylindrical type often comes to mind frst. This is the oldest battery technology, produced using a winding method similar to rolling a toilet paper roll, involving the coiling of the cathode and anode. Many manufacturers have already installed such large, highspeed manufacturing lines, enabling them to produce large quantities quickly and at a lower cost. Since cylindrical batteries are extensively used in various felds, they are equipped with international standards and criteria. Cylindrical batteries include the 18650, 21700, and the recently prominent 46-series batteries, such as the 4680 and 4695. The names “18650”, “21700”, and “4680 0 ” not only represent the type of cylindrical batteries but also their specifcations, with the “18650 battery” indicating a cylindrical shape with a diameter of 18 mm and a height of 65 mm. Generally, the larger the battery, the greater its energy storage capacity. Price Competitiveness and Rapid Production as Advantages Cylindrical Battery 18650, 21700, 46 Series The Standard of Cylindrical Batteries, 18650 The journey of cylindrical batteries began in the early 1990s with Japan’s commercialization efforts. Initially, “14500” and “20500” batteries were developed as secondary batteries to replace primary batteries, which are used only once. Although they were produced in sizes compatible with AA batteries such as nickel-cadmium, and nickel-metal hydride batteries, they didn’t gain popularity in the market as they weren’t suitable for camcorders, the main product using secondary batteries at that time. Consequently, considering the size of a camcorder that can be comfortably held in one hand, the 18650 battery, with a diameter of 18 mm and a height of 65 mm, was developed. The 18650 battery later gained enormous popularity, especially in the 2000s, fnding its way into laptops Part 4. Understanding the Current State of the Battery Industry ㅣ Types and Applications of Batteries64

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 65
articles.content_text: The Dark Horse of Cylindrical Batteries, 21700 The cylindrical battery, which had been experiencing a stagnant phase, regained attention in the mid-2010s when it began to be used in battery packs for electric bicycles, electric bikes, and golf carts. With the rise of the “micro-mobility” market, enabling short-distance travel, the demand for cylindrical batteries surged again, ushering in a second golden age. It was during this time that the 21700 battery emerged. To power an electric vehicle, thousands of 18650 batteries were required, making the vehicle heavier and reducing energy eff ciency. This led to a growing need for a battery with a larger capacity and higher energy density than the 18650. As a response, the 21700 cylindrical battery, measuring 21 mm in diameter and 70 mm in height, was developed. The 21700 enlarged the cell volume while increasing its capacity by over 50% by using cathode materials like NCM811 or NCMA, which contain over 80% nickel. As a result, the 21700 battery can power an electric vehicle with about 50% fewer batteries compared to the 18650. Additionally, the 21700 has the advantage of being manufactured on the existing 18650 production lines, eliminating the need for additional expansion. As LG Energy Solution shifted its focus to medium and large IT devices like power tools and vacuum cleaners, it diversif ed the forms of batteries. Moreover, by expanding into areas like electric vehicles, large-capacity energy storage systems ESS , and light electric vehicles LEVs such as electric bikes and electric scooters, cylindrical batteries are gaining attention as allrounder batteries. and mobile phones, thereby signif cantly driving the growth of the small battery market. At that time, LG Chem, the predecessor of LG Energy Solution, began mass-producing the world’s f rst cylindrical lithium-ion battery for laptops 2,200 mAh in 2001, starting the trend of integrating cylindrical batteries into small IT devices. From 900 million cells in 2002, the battery market grew to 3.1 billion cells by 2008, achieving an annual growth rate of 24%. Although small in size and light in weight, the 18650 battery emerged as a market leader with its high energy density, durability, and the advantage of standardized sizes enabling mass production and competitive pricing. However, in the early 2010s, as IT devices became smaller and thinner, the market for cylindrical batteries entered a period of stagnation. ENSOLPEDIA65

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 66
articles.content_text: Recently, the spotlight in the cylindrical battery sector has been on the 46-series models. These batteries, with a diameter of 46mm and varying lengths, are designed to achieve greater energy density and power output. According to SNE Research, demand for 4680 batteries in xEVs is expected to grow rapidly, reaching approximately 72 GWh in 2025 and around 650 GWh by 2030. Therefore, competition in the battery market is expected to intensify, focusing on achieving greater capacity and higher energy density. Part 4. Understanding the Current State of the Battery Industry ㅣ Types and Applications of Batteries66

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 67
articles.content_text: A formidable challenge in the battery industry is the development of “thinner, lighter batteries with higher energy density”. The pouch-type battery, which involves encasing the cathode, anode, and electrolyte in a metallic pouch, is seen as one solution to this long-standing issue. Unlike cylindrical batteries, which are made by rolling materials, pouch-type batteries stack the cathode, anode, and separator layer by layer inside an aluminum foil pouch. This structure allows for minimal space wastage, resulting in higher energy density. Moreover, due to their flm material packaging, pouch-type batteries can be made in a variety of sizes and shapes, unlike cylindrical and prismatic batteries, making them suitable for diverse applications in electric vehicles and IT devices. However, they require advanced technology and involve a complex manufacturing process, leading to higher production costs. Additionally, a process called “degassing” is necessary to remove gases generated during the activation/formation phases of the battery. LG Energy Solution has developed a degassing method for more efficient production of pouchtype batteries, which involves holding the battery cells vertically to remove gas. The conventional degassing process for pouch-type batteries involves lying the cells horizontally inside a chamber, which unavoidably leads to some electrolyte leakage and necessitates additional electrolytes to be added. By applying its proprietary degassing method, LG Energy Solution can effectively remove gas, thereby improving manufacturing effciency and cost competitiveness. Pouch-type batteries ranked second in market share 26% in the EV battery market in 2022, following prismatic batteries, and experts predict their market share will increase to 31% by 2030. Their high energy density and versatile applications make them particularly appealing. High-Density Energy Battery that Fits in Your Pocket Pouch-Type Battery Pouch-type batteries have a high energy density because they can fll space without gaps. They are also highly versatile as they can be easily manufactured in the desired shape. ENSOLPEDIA67

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 68
articles.content_text: Energy Density 250350Wh/L Energy Density 450-550Wh/L EVs running on the road 150200km Improving battery and mileage 200400km Cathode Nickel about 33% Cathode Nickel about 60-70% Charging Time Quick charging in 60 minutes 80% Charging Time Quick charging in 40 minutes 80% Anode Natural or artif cial graphite Anode Natural and artif cial graphite 1st 20102016 2nd 20162021 Electric vehicles EVs , which operate by obtaining electrical energy from batteries, can be classified into three types based on the role of the battery“HEV Hybrid Electric Vehicle ”, “PHEV Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle ”, and “BEV Battery Electric Vehicle ”. The battery in an HEV plays a supporting role for the engine, enhancing the output when the electric motor adds power during driving. HEVs use durable batteries that have high power and performance such as nickel-metal hydride or NCA Nickel·Cobalt·Aluminum batteries. The primary method of charging these batteries is through regenerative braking, where kinetic energy is converted into electrical energy for charging the battery during deceleration when the brakes are applied while driving. PHEVs are equipped with batteries that are about 4 to 10 times larger than those in HEVs. Unlike HEVs where the battery plays a supporting role, in PHEVs, the lithium-ion battery serves as the primary power source, with the engine serving a secondary role. Hence, PHEVs require largercapacity batteries. While some types of PHEVs can be charged through regenerative braking, their primary charging method typically involves using an external power source, such as connecting to a charger plugging in BEVs operate solely on battery power, making them true “electric vehicles”. Thus, they use larger capacity batteries with higher energy density than HEVs and PHEVs. BEVs typically utilize batteries of 60 Ah or more and use cathode materials such as NCM Nickel·Cobalt·Manganese or LMO Lithium Manganese Oxide to enhance energy density. Furthermore, LG Energy Solution is actively engaged in the research and development of pouch-type battery cells to produce batteries that are high in energy and performance, optimized for vehicle design. The batteries in BEVs have seen gradual improvements in driving range and charging time thanks to technological advancements, leading to their classif cation into different generations. For instance, the f rst-generation BEVs could charge up to 80% in an hour and cover a distance of 150200 km, and the second-generation could travel 200400 km on a single charge, with the charging time reduced to 40 minutes. Also, the recently introduced third-generation BEVs can charge up to 80% in less than 30 minutes and travel more than 500 km. Classifying Electric Vehicles EVs Based on Battery Role HEV·PHEV·BEV Part 4. Understanding the Current State of the Battery Industry ㅣ Types and Applications of Batteries68

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 69
articles.content_text: Energy Density 650750Wh/L Cathode Nickel over 80% Charging Time Quick charging in 30 minutes 80% Anode Natural and artif cial graphite, silicon Next generation EVs competing with internal combustion engine cars 500km3rd 2021Check As countries worldwide recognize the importance of carbon neutrality and push for stronger ESG-related policies, internal combustion engine vehicles are rapidly being replaced by eco-friendly electric vehicles EVs This shift has accelerated the evolution of EVs. From 2010 to 2016, the first-generation EVs had a driving range of 150200 km on a single charge, which was considered insuff cient. The battery charging time for these vehicles was also about 60 minutes to reach 80% capacity using rapid charging, with an energy density of 250350 Wh/L. Plus, the nickel content in the cathode materials of these batteries was approximately 33%, and the anode materials primarily used natural or synthetic graphite. The second-generation EVs, released between 2016 and 2021, significantly improved upon the battery performance and driving range of the first generation, thereby playing a crucial role in increasing EV demand. The driving range of these vehicles was 200400 km on a single charge, roughly double that of the f rst generation. The battery energy density improved to 450550 Wh/L, and the rapid charging time was reduced to about 40 minutes for an 80% charge. This was achieved by increasing the nickel content in the cathode materials to 6070%. The third-generation EVs, appearing after 2021, further extended the driving range to over 500 km, overcoming the range limitations of previous generations. The battery charging time was also reduced to 30 minutes for an 80% charge using rapid charging, almost half the time compared to the f rst generation. The energy density of these batteries increased to 650–750 Wh/L. Moreover, the nickel content in the cathode materials was raised to over 80%, and the anode began to include silicon-based materials in addition to natural and synthetic graphite. Driving Range Increases Proportionally with Battery Technology Development! Characteristics of Electric Vehicles by Generation ENSOLPEDIA69

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 70
articles.content_text: As the electric vehicle EV market grows, the generation of end-of-life batteries is increasing rapidly. Bloomberg NEF, a global market research frm, predicts that by 2032, over 110 GWh of end-of-life batteries will be generated. This is equivalent to the energy needed for 11 million EVs traveling an average of 50 km per day. Battery manufacturers are therefore continuously contemplating planning and working to reduce greenhouse gas emissions in the battery production process, as well as to prevent resource depletion and minimize environmental pollution. Efforts Towards a Carbon-Neutral Society Reuse and Recycling of End-of-Life Batteries Reuse and Recycling of End-of-Life Batteries The primary use of end-of-life batteries are reuse and recycling. “Reuse” involves collecting batteries that have some remaining life after their use in EVs, processing them, and repurposing them for various applications. On the other hand, “recycling” refers to using batteries that are beyond reuse as raw materials in the production of new batteries. The first step in battery reuse involves collecting end-of-life batteries through regional collection systems and selecting those suitable for reuse based on their state of health SoH and state of charge SoC Once suitable batteries are identified, solutions are developed to meet the quality standards and certifcation regulations for their intended use, thereby ensuring their competitiveness as reused products. In addition, following the sale of reused battery products, a system is designed to facilitate their collection for future recycling. In contrast, recycling of batteries involves disassembling and dissolving end-of-life batteries that are no longer usable, extracting materials like cobalt and nickel, and reintroducing them into the production of cathode materials for new batteries. This process is part of establishing a circular economy that encompasses the entire value chain, from raw material production to consumption and disposal. LG Energy Solution’s Eforts to Establish a Circular System Amid global efforts to achieve carbon neutrality, national policies on battery utilization are also expanding. Especially, along with the rapid growth of electric vehicle market, the global end-of-life battery market is expected to grow to approximately KRW 20 trillion by 2030 and KRW 60 trillion by 2050. In response, various companies, including local governments in South Korea, are focusing on end-of-life batteries. Part 4. Understanding the Current State of the Battery Industry ㅣ Types and Applications of Batteries70

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 71
articles.content_text: LG Energy Solution is also committed to preventing environmental pollution and creating sustainable value. It is continuously developing techniques to accurately assess the remaining life of batteries and predict their usable lifespan, and is exploring business models that utilize end-of-life batteries in collaboration with various organizations. Moreover, LG Energy Solution is working on establishing a circular system that involves disassembling, refning, and smelting batteries that can no longer be reused and using them as cathode materials. In this regard, in May 2021, LG Energy Solution entered a recycling contract with Li-Cycle, North America’s largest battery recycling company, through Ultium Cells, a joint venture with General Motors GM Ultium Cells plans to recycle various battery raw materials such as cobalt, nickel, lithium, graphite, copper, manganese, and aluminum through this contract. In June 2021, LG Energy Solution demonstrated the value of battery reuse by establishing an EV charging ESS System Reuse Battery Project at its Ochang plant in Chungbuk, South Korea. This system, made from batteries retrieved from EV taxis that have driven over 100,000 km, reduces charging costs and can charge a GM electric vehicle, such as the Bolt, for 300 km in one hour using a 100 kW charger. ENSOLPEDIA71

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 72
articles.content_text: Composition and Key Roles of ESS An Energy Storage System ESS is a system that stores produced electricity and supplies it when needed, thereby enhancing energy effciency. It mainly consists of four componentsa battery, a power conversion system PCS , a battery management system BMS , and an energy management system EMS The PCS is a power conversion device that converts alternating current to direct current and vice versa. The BMS is a battery management system that controls the numerous battery cells in the ESS as one unit and includes safety features that stop charging and discharging if abnormalities in voltage, current, or temperature are detected. The EMS is a system that monitors the amount of electricity stored in the ESS and serves as software responsible for the overall operation of the ESS. There are three primary roles of ESS. First, it acts as an uninterrupted power supply in places where power outages must be avoided at all costs, such as hospitals and banks. In cases of power outages caused by typhoons or fres, it assists the power supply to mitigate the large-scale blackout situations. Second, it stores generated energy and supplies it during peak power demand times. It helps stabilize energy output by compensating for the inconsistent generation levels of new renewable energy such as solar and wind power. In homes, it reduces electricity costs by storing electricity during cheaper hours and using it during more expensive daytime hours. Third, it maintains the standard power frequency at 60 Hz in the power supply system, countering frequency fuctuations due to mismatches in supply and demand, thereby enhancing power quality. Global Attention on ESS A 2024 report by SNE Research projects global ESS demand to grow from 235 GWh in 2024 to 618 GWh by 2035. Wood Mackenzie, a research company, identifed ESS as one of the most promising technologies for Europe to achieve its carbon emission reduction targets by 2030. Major countries like the United States, China, and the United Kingdom are swiftly moving to dominate the ESS market. The United States is one of the leading ESS markets that has expanded through the step-by-step development of policies and institutional frameworks. In 2020, the Energy Storage Grand Challenge Roadmap introduced ESS installation mandates across major states, and deployment has since accelerated through tax incentives and continued policy support. China has positioned ESS as a key element for power system stability under its 15th Five-Year Plan period from 2026 to 2030. The Guiding Opinions on Promoting Renewable Energy Consumption and Regulation place greater emphasis on grid integration and power system quality beyond capacity expansion, while driving the ESS sector toward greater technological and operational competitiveness. Germany is advancing its energy transition with a goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2045. In a Building a Stable Power Storage Supply System! The ESS Industry and Secondary Batteries Part 4. Understanding the Current State of the Battery Industry ㅣ Types and Applications of Batteries72

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 73
articles.content_text: system with a high share of renewable energy, ESS serves as core infrastructure to stabilize power supply and demand while improving overall system effciency. Australia is expanding investment in renewable energy and ESS under the Capacity Investment Scheme CIS The country is increasing support and advancing the deployment of large-scale facilities, including solar, wind, and ESS, by 2030. Japan plans to raise the share of renewable energy to 40-50% by 2040 under its 7th Strategic Energy Plan. It also aims to expand the use of ESS, supported by a diversifed power mix. ESS and NEC Energy Solutions South Korea continues to strengthen its competitiveness in the ESS industry through diverse initiatives. LG Energy Solution is developing ESS batteries based on high-loading and high-density cell technologies to meet growing demand driven by the expansion of renewable energy, while providing solutions applicable across a wide range of sectors including grid, residential, commercial, and UPS. Through its subsidiary LG Energy Solution Vertech, the company has secured end-to-end ESS solution capabilities that go beyond battery supply to include system design, operation, and maintenance. It has also strengthened its business competitiveness following the acquisition of NEC Energy Solutions, a specialized ESS system integrator. In addition, by leveraging its ESS-dedicated software AEROS™, LG Energy Solution enhances operational effciency through data analysis and real-time monitoring, while strengthening its presence in the global market. The company has signed an 8 GWh ESS supply agreement with Terra-Gen and plans to supply batteries for an approximately 1 GWh ESS project in collaboration with PGE in Poland. Furthermore, through cooperation with Delta Electronics, it is advancing the supply of residential ESS batteries totaling 4 GWh between 2025 and 2030, continuing to expand its business across grid, commercial, and residential ESS markets. Country Key policy and investment initiatives U.S. ESS installation mandates, tax benefts, and ESS inclusion in RPS Various policies implemented by the federal and state governments China Broader ESS deployment during the 15th Five-Year Plan Renewable energy capacity expansion and improved grid integration capability and power system quality Germany Transition to a carbon-neutral energy system ESS as core infrastructure, with applications expanding into areas such as heating and transportation Australia Increased investment in renewable energy and ESS under the CIS Plans to deploy approximately 40 GW of capacity by 2030 Japan Higher share of renewable energy under the 7th Strategic Energy Plan Power mix diversifcation and enhanced power system stability ENSOLPEDIA73

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 76
articles.content_text: 76 Part 5. Examining the Battery Industry that Will Dominate the Future ㅣ Next-Generation Technologies and Process Innovation Maximized Lightness with Pouch-Type Design Lithium-Sulfur Battery How Lithium-Sulfur Batteries Work Since the commercialization of lithium-ion batteries in 1991, the scope of secondary battery applications has signif cantly expanded, leading to more diverse characteristics of batteries required by different industries. As a result, research has been conducted to develop the next-generation battery that is more advanced than lithium-ion batteries, and one of the key contenders that emerged from this is the lithium-sulfur Li-S battery. Interestingly, Li-S batteries were actually developed even earlier than lithium-ion batteries. In 1962, American scientists Herbert Danuta and Ulam Juliusz f led a patent for a primary battery using sulfur as the cathode and lithium as the anode. However, due to several technical challenges, it was not commercially applicable at the time. It was only after the introduction of lithium nitrate LiNO3 that Li-S batteries started to function as secondary batteries. Li-S batteries operate by using a sulfur-carbon composite as the cathode and lightweight lithium metal as the anode, generating electrical energy through a process called “stepwise sulfur conversion.” During discharge, the eight sulfur atoms joined together in the form of a ring S8 undergo a series of reduction reactions, sequentially broken down into linear structures known as lithium polysulfide. Lithium polysulf de is an intermediate compound formed when lithium combines with sulfur. It moves from the cathode to the anode while dissolved in the electrolyte and continues to undergo reduction reactions. Finally, the lithium polysulf de transforms into lithium sulf de Li2S , an insoluble compound, generating electrical energy. Reduction reactions take place until one sulfur atom can bind with two lithium ions, allowing for a high amount of lithium oxidation. During charging, the reverse oxidation reaction occurs, converting lithium sulf de back into sulfur. 1. Gravimetric Energy Density Wh/kg = Electrical Energy Wh Battery Weight kg Lightweighting through High Gravimetric Energy Density The key feature of Li-S batteries is associated with weight, which is closely related to their gravimetric energy density Wh/kg 1. The gravimetric energy density, also known as specific energy, measures how much energy is stored per unit weight of a battery, calculated by dividing the battery’s energy Wh by its weight kg Batteries that can store more energy while remaining lightweight are undoubtedly more efficient, and Li-S batteries are characterized by their high gravimetric energy density. As the sulfur-carbon composite for the cathode and the lithium metal for the anode have low density and high capacity per weight, the Li-S battery’s gravimetric energy density is more than 1.5 times higher than the conventional lithium-ion battery. This means that lithium-sulfur batteries are lightweight. While lithium-ion batteries use heavy metals like nickel and cobalt for their cathodes, Li-S batteries utilize much lighter materials—sulfur and lithium— resulting in a weight reduction. If the capacity generated per gram of active material in a lightweight Sulfur Carbon Electrolyte Cathode Sulfur-carbon composites Anode Separator Lithiummetal

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 77
articles.content_text: 77 2. A method that applies high energy, such as extremely high temperature and pressure, to a material to induce chemical reactions or material synthesis. battery is high, it should be considered as a crucial factor when developing and manufacturing secondary batteries. For sulfur, the cathode material in Li-S batteries, the capacity can reach 1,675 mAh/g, whereas ternary lithium-ion batteries NCM only provide about 200 mAh/g. This alone shows that Li-S batteries are roughly eight times lighter than lithium-ion batteries. Additionally, the lithium in the anode of a Li-S battery has a capacity of 3,500mAh/g, compared to 350mAh/g for the graphite anode in a lithium-ion battery. Being the 17th most abundant element on Earth, sulfur is relatively easy to source and very affordable, providing Li-S batteries with a competitive cost advantage when used as the cathode material. Thanks to their lightweight and high energy density, Li-S batteries are drawing attention from the aerospace f eld for applications such as drones, unmanned aerial vehicles UAVs , and urban air mobility UAM vehicles. It is because their reduced weight enhances fuel eff ciency, and the high energy density can signif cantly increase the f ight duration and distance compared to lithium-ion batteries. In fact, in 2020, LG Energy Solution integrated Li-S batteries into a high-altitude, long-endurance solarpowered UAV developed by the Korea Aerospace Research Institute and successfully completed a 13-hour test f ight at the highest altitude in the stratosphere. LG Energy Solution has proven that it has the world-class Li-S battery technology by demonstrating the f ight’s stable performance in extreme conditions with temperatures as low as -70°C and atmospheric pressure just 1/25th of that of the ground level. LG Energy Solution Leads Technology Innovation through Continuous Research LG Energy Solution recently presented new possibilities for next-generation high-capacity batteries through its sulfur-based solid-state electrolyte SSE technology, which fundamentally suppresses the polysulf de dissolution environment. This research, conducted jointly with Professor Shirley Meng of the University of Chicago, a globally recognized expert in solid-state batteries, is particularly signif cant in that it achieved high energy storage performance by applying sulfur as a cathode material. In particular, the team applied a high-energy synthesis approach2 to form a highly ion-conductive interphase on the surface of sulfur particles, while controlling the particle size at the micrometer μm level. This enabled improvements in both cycle stability and power performance. As a result, the battery maintained stable charge–discharge operation at room temperature 25°C and achieved a high areal capacity of up to 11 mAh/cm². Furthermore, the study proposed various high-energy-density cell designs and demonstrated practical applicability by implementing a lithium sulf de Li₂S -based anodeless cell in a pouch format that operates smoothly even under a relatively low pressure of 10 MPa. This approach enhances the technology’s potential for industrial commercialization. Based on these achievements, LG Energy Solution plans to accelerate the development of nextgeneration battery technologies that combine safety, high energy density, and cost competitiveness.ENSOLPEDIA

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 78
articles.content_text: 78 Part 5. Examining the Battery Industry that Will Dominate the Future ㅣ Next-Generation Technologies and Process Innovation A New Perspective on Developing Anode Materials While significant advancements have been made in developing cathode materials for lithiumion batteries to enhance performance, the capacity per unit mass of the materials is nearing its theoretical limits. As a result, improving battery capacity through advancements in anode materials has emerged as a new breakthrough. Currently, graphite is the dominant anode material, with only small amounts of silicon added. Accordingly, lithium metal is being researched as a promising material. Lithium metal batteries use lithium metal as the anode, metal oxide-based materials similar to those found in lithium-ion batteries as the cathode, and liquid electrolytes. Lithium metal is used as an anode material in various next-generation batteries. In this case, when a sulfur-carbon composite is used for the cathode, it forms a lithium-sulfur battery, whereas using oxygen as the cathode results in a lithium-air battery. Lithium metal batteries are considered the original model of lithium-ion batteries. Professor Stanley Whittingham, who was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry, developed the f rst lithium-ion battery in the 1970s, using titanium disulf de TiS₂ as the cathode and lithium metal as the anode. However, due to safety concerns, it was not widely commercialized. The main issue was the formation of dendrites1— sharp, tree-like lithium structures that grow on the anode surface during charging. These dendrites can damage the separator and even grow to the surface of the cathode, causing the cathode and anode to come into direct contact, resulting in internal short circuits. In 1988, the Canadian company Moli Energy introduced the “Molicel,” a rechargeable battery that used molybdenum disulfide MoS₂ as the cathode and lithium metal as the anode. Though lithium metal batteries were on the verge of commercialization when they were installed in mobile phones by NTT, the Japanese telecommunications company, the safer lithium-ion batteries eventually became the mainstream in the market. Key Features of Lithium Metal Batteries Lithium metal batteries, considered one of the leading next-generation batteries, offer several distinct advantages. First, lithium metal batteries can achieve higher energy density than lithium-ion batteries that use graphite as the anode. Graphite has a relatively low theoretical capacity 2 of 372mAh/g. In contrast, lithium metal offers a much higher theoretical capacity of 3,860mAh/g, over 10 times that of graphite. This 1. Refer to the “Battery Glossary” on page 111 for the term ‘Dendrite.’ 2. The maximum lithium-ion content that can be physically stored within the electrode material. Improving Capacity with Anode Material Innovation Lithium Metal Batteries Anode Graphite Anode Graphite Separator Separator Cathode Cathode Lithium-ion battery Anode Lithium metal Anode Lithium metal Separator Separator Cathode Cathode Lithium metal battery Anode currentcollector

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 79
articles.content_text: 79 means that lithium metal batteries can store signifcantly more energy in the same weight. Secondly, the battery’s volume can be reduced. Although lithium has a density of approximately 0.534g/ cm³ with a large volume per unit mass, it offers more than 10 times the specifc capacity of graphite, allowing for a thinner anode. As a result, space utilization is improved, allowing more electrical energy to be stored in the same space, thereby extending the operating time of devices powered by the battery. Additionally, this improved space effciency enables the implementation of high-power systems, such as autonomous vehicles, which require high energy consumption. Thirdly, it can achieve faster charging speeds compared to conventional lithium-ion batteries. In lithiumion batteries, lithium ions are stored by intercalating into the graphite layers during charging. However, in lithium metal batteries, lithium ions move to the anode and are directly reduced and electrodeposited onto the lithium metal surface, without the need to fnd gaps in the structure. This theoretically allows for faster charging speeds. Lastly, lithium metal batteries can use liquid electrolytes, utilizing the manufacturing process similar to that of lithium-ion batteries. This allows manufacturers to use part of the existing lithium-ion battery production equipment and optimize processes more quickly. Opening the Era of Fast Charging A joint research team between LG Energy Solution and KAIST FRL has developed a core technology that can dramatically reduce the charging time of lithium metal batteries, a nextgeneration battery technology. These batteries significantly enhance energy density and driving range. When applied to electric vehicles, they can deliver a driving range of over 800 km on a single charge, a lifespan exceeding 300,000 km, and charging times as short as 12 minutes. Until now, lithium metal batteries have faced challenges in achieving suffcient lifespan and safety due to dendrite formation. In particular, under fast-charging conditions, dendrite growth becomes more severe, leading to internal short circuits and hindering commercialization. The research team identifed that the root cause lies in the non-uniform interfacial aggregation on the lithium metal surface and, for the first time, developed a novel liquid electrolyte capable of suppressing this issue. It utilizes an anion structure with weak binding affnity to lithium ions, minimizing interfacial non-uniformity and effectively suppressing dendrite growth. As a result, it overcomes the limitations of slow charging and enables stable operation. This study builds on lithium metal battery research based on the “low-corrosivity borate-pyran” liquid electrolyte announced in 2023 and represents a meaningful step toward the commercialization of nextgeneration batteries by securing fast-charging technology, one of the most critical challenges. Going forward, LG Energy Solution will continue to strengthen industry-academia collaboration with leading universities at home and abroad to address key technological challenges in next-generation battery development.
