articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 80
articles.content_text: 80 Part 5. Examining the Battery Industry that Will Dominate the Future ㅣ Next-Generation Technologies and Process Innovation More Advanced High-Voltage Mid-Nickel Batteries LG Energy Solution categorizes electric vehicles into three segmentsPremium high-priced , Standard mid-low-priced , and Affordable low-priced It develops batteries that are optimal for each segment based on criteria such as price, charging time, and driving range. Out of them, the mid-nickel battery is designed for vehicles under the “Standard” segment. According to a 2030 global EV demand outlook published by EV Volumes, a global market intelligence platform, the Standard segment will account for 66%, Premium for 19%, and Affordable for 15% of the global demand. Due to the high demand for vehicles under the Standard segment in the entire EV market, the signif cance of mid-nickel batteries is becoming increasingly apparent. A mid-nickel battery refers to a battery composed of nickel, cobalt, and manganese in various proportions, with a nickel content ranging from 40% to 60%. Batteries with a nickel content of 80% or higher are classified as high-nickel batteries. Recently, high-nickel products with a nickel content exceeding 90% have also emerged. The current focus is on developing high-voltage mid-nickel batteries, which differ from earlier versions of mid-nickel batteries developed before the emergence of high-nickel batteries. These high-voltage mid-nickel batteries are characterized by reduced nickel content while increasing the proportion of manganese, resulting in improved safety and higher energy density1. The relatively reduced energy capacity due to the decrease in nickel content is compensated through high voltage. Another advantage of mid-nickel batteries is cost efficiency. Mid-nickel batteries have a reduced proportion of expensive materials like nickel and cobalt while containing a higher proportion of manganese, which is more affordable, thus leading to lower production costs. Additionally, mid-nickel batteries can use less expensive lithium carbonate instead of lithium hydroxide, further enhancing cost competitiveness. Since nickel is diff cult to synthesize with lithium at high temperatures, when the nickel content is high, lithium hydroxide, which has a lower melting point than lithium carbonate, is typically used. However, as mid-nickel batteries have lower nickel content, lithium carbonate can be used instead. 1. Energy Density = Voltage × Capacity Current × Time Weight Ni LiNiO2 Ni Co LiCoO2 Mn LiMnO2 Mn Co Reduced Costs and Enhanced Safety High-Voltage Mid-NickelBatteries

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 81
articles.content_text: 81 Single-Crystal Cathode Materials, the Key to High-Voltage Mid-Nickel Batteries While implementing high voltage in mid-nickel batteries can enhance energy density, it can also cause cracks in the material, which may shorten the battery’s lifespan. To overcome this challenge, LG Energy Solution has applied single-crystal cathode technology to address these challenges. Currently, poly-crystal cathode materials, where small metal particles are agglomerated to form multiple crystals, are widely used. In contrast, primary particles form a single-crystalline structure in single-crystal cathode materials. This means that metals such as nickel, cobalt, and manganese, used in the cathode of a lithium-ion battery, are consolidated into a one-body particle shape. Single-crystal cathode materials offer the advantage of causing fewer cracks under high pressure. Research also shows that single-crystal cathode materials can increase energy capacity by about 10% and extend battery life by around 30% compared to poly-crystal cathode materials. LG Energy Solution is applying this technology to produce high-quality, high-voltage mid-nickel batteries, and is also using advanced coating methods to better control the reactions of active materials. Diversif cation of LG Energy Solution’s Battery Lineup To proactively respond to global EV market trends, LG Energy Solution aims to begin mass production of high-voltage mid-nickel NCM batteries by 2025. These batteries are expected to increase energy density to 670Wh/L, improve cell stability by over 30% compared to high-nickel batteries, and reduce prices by approximately 8%. In addition, LG Energy Solution was the f rst in the industry to apply 100% mid-nickel pure to laptop batteries, and its “Mid-Nickel Pure NCM” battery won the “Best Innovator Award” at the 2024 InterBattery Awards, further validating its quality. This battery is equipped with the mid-nickel material “NCM613,” capable of operating at high voltage. The NCM613, a f agship model of high-voltage mid-nickel batteries, consists of 60% nickel, 10% cobalt, and 30% manganese. It features single-crystal cathode materials, ensuring durability in high-voltage environments. 60% 10% 30% Polycrystalline Material Poly-Crystal Cathodes Single-crystalline Material Single-Crystal Cathodes Charging/ Discharging and Rolling Preventing CracksENSOLPEDIA

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 82
articles.content_text: 82 Part 5. Examining the Battery Industry that Will Dominate the Future ㅣ Next-Generation Technologies and Process Innovation The Ultimate Battery with Enhanced Safety and Energy Density All-solid-state batteries are being researched as a solution to overcome the theoretical limitations of lithiumion batteries. Having a solid electrolyte, these batteries eliminate the risk of electrolyte leakage from external shocks and offer high thermal stability, signif cantly reducing the likelihood of f res. Due to their enhanced safety, space previously allocated for thermal management systems can be minimized, allowing more cells to f t within the battery pack. This ultimately contributes to an extended driving range for electric vehicles EVs In an all-solid-state battery, where all components are solid, the solid electrolyte can also function as a separator, further enhancing performance. Typically, solid materials are denser than liquids of the same volume, allowing for increased energy density by f lling the space previously occupied by the separator with additional cathode and anode active materials. Additionally, the solid electrolyte is less affected by external temperature changes, contributing to addressing the common issue of reduced battery performance in colder conditions. However, the lower ion conductivity and higher interfacial1 resistance compared to liquid electrolytes are challenges that all-solid-state batteries must overcome. In the case of liquid electrolytes, surface tension allows close adhesion to the electrodes, facilitating the easy movement of lithium ions. However, with solid electrolytes, lithium-ion diffusion and the redox reactions of active materials occur only at contact points where the two materials meet. Consequently, uneven distribution of the solid electrolyte can increase interfacial resistance, potentially hindering lithium-ion movement. Therefore, research efforts are ongoing to address these challenges. Three Types of Solid Electrolytes 1. The boundary that forms between two phases—such as gas, liquid, or solid—where they come into contact. High-Density Battery Using Solid Electrolytes as Separator Replacements All-Solid-State Batteries 1. Sulfide-based Sulfide-based electrolytes are considered the most promising solid electrolyte material, as they form a wide contact interface between the electrode and electrolyte, resulting in very high ion conductivity and uniform interfacial resistance. However, hydrogen sulfide H₂S gas is produced during processing, and it is sensitive to moisture, requiring strict humidity control throughout all processing steps. 2. Oxide Although oxide-based electrolytes have lower ion conductivity than sulfide-based ones, they offer excellent electrochemical stability in air. However, because forming a contact interface between the electrolyte and the electrode is challenging, hightemperature heat treatment is required for synthesis. 3. Polymer Polymer-based electrolytes are characterized by their hybrid compatibility with various electrolytes, including both liquid and solid forms, and can leverage existing liquid electrolyte manufacturing technologies, thereby reducing manufacturing costs. However, they have lower ion conductivity compared to sulfide and oxide materials, which can lead to decreased performance and power output at low temperatures, prompting ongoing research to address theselimitations.

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 83
articles.content_text: 83 Anode Materials Used in All-Solid-State Batteries Lithium Metal Anodes Utilizing lithium metal as the anode material in a battery can increase capacity by about ten times compared to graphite, significantly boosting energy density. This is because lithium metal can store more lithium ions released from the cathode. Additionally, lithium ions moving to the anode directly undergo a reduction reaction with lithium metal, leading to faster charging rates. For these reasons, lithium metal anodes are regarded as a next-generation battery material. However, a known issue with lithium metal anodes is the formation of lithium crystals, called dendrites, on the surface during charge and discharge cycles, which reduces battery capacity and charging/discharging efficiency. In all-solid-state batteries, the solid electrolyte acts as a robust separator, helping to prevent cell damage that dendrites might cause. Silicon Anodes Silicon anodes, which have a significantly higher energy capacity than graphite anodes, which, therefore, enhances energy density, can also be used in all-solid-state batteries. However, due to the potential for considerable volume changes that can occur during charging and discharging, research is underway to develop methods for effectively incorporating larger amounts of silicon into the anode. LG Energy Solution’s Success in Developing Long-Lasting All-Solid-State Batteries In 2021, LG Energy Solution formed a joint research team with the University of California, San Diego UCSD to advance the research and development of all-solid-state batteries. As a result, they addressed the technical limitations of existing all-solid-state batteries, which previously required charging at temperatures above 60°C, by developing a “long-lasting all-solid-state battery” capable of rapid charging even at room temperature around 25°C The key breakthrough was the application of “micro-silicon anode material” with a particle size of approximately 5 μm in the battery’s anode. Silicon anodes are known to be challenging to implement due to substantial volume changes during charge and discharge cycles. Despite these challenges, the battery achieved over 500 charge-discharge cycles at room temperature, retaining more than 80% of its capacity afterward. Additionally, energy density improved by over 40% compared to currently commercialized lithium-ion batteries, making a major advancement. This research was published in Science Vol. 373, Issue 6562 , a prestigious scientif c journal, receiving global recognition for its signif cance.ENSOLPEDIA

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 84
articles.content_text: 84 Part 5. Examining the Battery Industry that Will Dominate the Future ㅣ Next-Generation Technologies and Process Innovation Cell-to-Pack, Enhancing Energy Density and Capacity through Space Ef ciency The operating voltage of a typical battery cell ranges from approximately 2.5 to 4.5V. For applications that require high capacity and high power output, such as electric vehicles EVs and energy storage systems ESS , multiple battery cells are modularized and connected in series or parallel to form a battery pack. The power output and energy capacity of the battery pack are determined by the number of cells and modules it contains. Thus, the technology to configure as many cells and modules as possible in a battery pack is crucial for developing high-performance batteries. LG Energy Solution identified the growing demand for high-performance batteries and has been developing various technologies to suit the demand. Such effort was first represented by the development of the Module Pack Integrated MPI technology that integrates modules and packs. After continued research and development, LG Energy Solution unveiled a groundbreaking, innovative technology at the “InterBattery 2024” exhibition, held from March 6 to 8 at COEX in Seoul. It was the remarkable “Cell-to-Pack CTP ” technology which eliminates the modular stage, an intermediate process in a battery manufacturing process, and directly assembles cells into the battery pack. By utilizing CTP, the space typically occupied by module cases can be f lled with additional battery cells. This means that by minimizing dead space within the pack and securing additional space, the new design can improve energy density and capacity. Furthermore, eliminating the modular process can reduce the number of components required compared to the existing process, resulting in shortened manufacturing time and lowered costs. Meanwhile due to the absence of modules to secure and protect the cells, it is crucial to ensure the safety of CTP batteries through impact absorption and other protective methods. Thus, securing both safety and performance is being researched as a major task along with optimizing the CTP process and developing advanced thermal protection TP solutions. LG Energy Solution, Industry’s First to Implement CTP for Pouch-Type Batteries LG Energy Solution’s CTP technology is garnering signif cant attention as the company is the first in the battery industry to apply the technology to pouch-type batteries. LG Energy Solution focused on the advantages of pouchtype cells of being lightweight and having minimal dead space when stacked, and actively utilized such features. The manufacturing process of pouch-type CTP batteries starts by arranging one or more stacked pouch-type cells and attaching compression pads to their sides. These cells are Innovative Technology that Eliminates the Modular Process Cell-to-Pack CTP Arrange one or more stacked pouch-type battery cells and attach compression pads to the sides. Apply thermal resin to the pack case and place the cell units inside. Connect the battery cells with busbars to make a cell unit. Install the Battery Managemen System BMS on the batterypack.

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 85
articles.content_text: 85 then electrically connected through a busbar and enclosed in a cell cover, forming a cell unit. The cell cover serves to protect the pouch-type battery cells from external shocks and helps maintain a stable stacking conf guration. After assembling the cell unit, thermal resin is applied to the pack case. The cell units are then placed, and the thermal resin enhances heat transfer between the pack case and the bottom plate, boosting the cooling efficiency of the battery cells. Finally, the Battery Management System BMS , which monitors and controls various battery data, is installed to complete the process. Ongoing Research for More Advanced CTP Implementation Currently, LG Energy Solution’s pouch-type CTP Cell-to-Pack batteries can be engineered to offer approximately 5% higher energy density per weight compared to prismatic CTP batteries. This enables LG Energy Solution to provide customized solutions that enhance energy eff ciency for each customer’s vehicle. LG Energy Solution continues to advance its CTP pack products incorporating pouch-type battery cells and has significantly streamlined the CTP process compared to MTP Module to Pack through process innovation. In addition, LG Energy Solution has established a CTP pilot line with the goal of developing and validating new processes for more advanced CTP implementation. The pilot line is actively identifying and researching CTP process innovation tasks across various areas, including assembly, welding, resin application, and inspection. articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 86
articles.content_text: 86 Part 5. Examining the Battery Industry that Will Dominate the Future ㅣ Next-Generation Technologies and Process Innovation What is the Dry Electrode Process? Electrode manufacturing is a process to produce the cathode and anode, which is the first and crucial step in battery production. This process comprises a sequence of stepsmixing, coating, roll pressing, and slitting & notching. Currently, most battery manufacturers choose the wet electrode process. In this method, cathode and anode active materials, conductive agents, and binders are mixed with a solvent to create a liquid slurry. The cathode and anode slurries are then thinly coated onto current collectors, each of the aluminum foil and copper foil, respectively. The solvent is dried in an oven at temperatures exceeding 100℃, completing the coating process. In contrast, the dry electrode process employs a dry method for both mixing and coating. This method is characterized by making a solid powder by mixing the cathode and anode active materials, conductive agents, and binders without a solvent during the mixing process. While several methods for coating the powder are being researched, one of the primary methods is the “maxwell method,” which involves laminating the powder in the form of a f lm onto the current collector, and the “powder coating method,” where the powder is sprinkled onto the current collector and then rolled. Dry Electrode Process Combining active materials, conductive agents, and binders Mixing Producing a solid powder mixture Solid Powder Formation Coating the solid powder onto the current collectors Solid Powder Coating Flattening and thinning the electrodes Roll Pressing Wet Electrode Process Active materials, conductive agents, binders, and solvents are combined Flattening and thinning the electrodes Mixing Drying in an oven at temperatures exceeding 100°C Solvent Drying Roll Pressing Forming a liquid slurry Slurry Formation Coating both cathode and anode slurries thinly onto the current collectors aluminum and copper foils Slurry Coating The Super-gap Technology that Eliminates a Dry Process Dry ElectrodeProcess

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 87
articles.content_text: 87 Reducing Manufacturing Costs While Increasing Energy Density The f rst advantage of the dry electrode process is its potential to drive cost innovation and achieve high productivity across the entire battery manufacturing process. Since it eliminates the need for a system facility to dry and recover solvents, it reduces equipment investment and manufacturing costs, as well as the size of the manufacturing space. Additionally, by skipping the drying step in the coating process, production speed and eff ciency are further enhanced. The second advantage is the potential for higher energy density compared to the wet process. In general, the thicker and denser the electrode, the higher the energy density. However, the traditional wet process has limitations in producing thick electrodes. When the slurry is coated thickly, a migration phenomenon occurs during the solvent drying stage, where conductive agents and binders move toward the surface of the electrode. Uneven distribution of conductive agents and binders can lead to several issues. In particular, if the conductive agents—which connect active materials to enable electrical properties—do not perform effectively, it can ultimately affect battery performance. On the other hand, the mixture of cathode and anode active materials, conductive agents, and binders produced in the dry electrode process is in solid form, which could make electrodes thicker. Since the dry method does not involve solvent drying, there is no migration phenomenon, allowing for uniform distribution of binders and conductive agents along the electrode thickness. As a result, it increases the amount of cathode and anode materials while reducing the proportion of separator and current collector, thereby achieving higher energy density. LG Energy Solution Leading the Commercialization of the Dry Electrode Process LG Energy Solution began developing dry electrode process technology about a decade ago, recognizing early on the need for solvent-free electrode manufacturing. Building on laboratory-stage results, the company has established a dry electrode pilot line at the Ochang Energy Plant and aims to secure mass production viability and commercialize the technology starting in 2028. Following commercialization, the technology will be gradually deployed at global facilities. By adopting the dry electrode process, the company expects to reduce electrode manufacturing costs by 17% to as much as 30%. Furthermore, LG Energy Solution is actively pursuing dry electrode applications for various active materials and collaborating to develop dry electrode processes for solid-state batteries, continuously leading technological innovation in the battery industry. Active material Conductive additive Even dispersion of binder, good connection between particles, relatively easy ion movement, outstanding cohesion with a lower but effective amount of binder DRY ELECTRODE BinderENSOLPEDIA

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 88
articles.content_text: 88 Part 5. Examining the Battery Industry that Will Dominate the Future ㅣ Next-Generation Technologies and Process Innovation Bipolar Battery that Enables High Voltage An eff cient battery must not only have high performance but also be price competitive. When applied to electric vehicles, this also means that technologies to increase driving range and reduce costs must be considered. While this is a focus on developing next-generation batteries, a keyword that has recently emerged is a bipolar battery with a series structure. When it comes to batteries, whether battery cells are connected in a series or a parallel structure is considered an important factor because they bring about differences in eff ciency and performance. A parallel connection refers to a method of connecting batteries by connecting the same poles— cathode to cathode and anode to anode—and then reconnecting them. When batteries are connected in parallel, the current f ows in multiple paths, making the battery structure complex. On the other hand, a series connection is a method of connecting batteries by connecting different poles—cathode to anode. When utilizing this arrangement, the current f ows in one path, making the current f ow simpler and the voltage higher than in a parallel connection. It is similar to how when multiple water tanks are connected as one, the water pressure increases and the water flows strongly. A bipolar battery is a battery that applies a series structure to the battery cell configuration. This simplifies the cell conf guration and increases energy density by implementing high voltage. Monopolar Battery VS Bipolar Battery In order to understand bipolar batteries, it is necessary to compare them with monopolar batteries. Lithium-ion batteries are classified into monopolar and bipolar structures based on the electrode structure. The monopolar structure that is currently widely used is unipolar, in which only one type of electrode, either a cathode or an anode, is located above and below the current collector. The cathode and anode slurries are applied to each cathode and anode current collector, respectively, and each electrode undergoes a welding process by the monocell unit to connect them in a parallel structure inside the cell. These independent cells in a parallel structure are connected again in a series structure through external electrical connectors to transfer electrons. This is the typical structure of the lithium- *ReferenceVISANG Education Parallel connection The strength of the water f ow is the same as when there is only one water container Series connection The water pressure increases, bolstering the strength of the water f ow Batteries in Series to Maximize Efficiency BipolarBattery

articles.title: All About Battery ENSOLPEDIA - page 89
articles.content_text: 89 Simplifcation of Pack Structure and Manufacturing Process The structural characteristic of the bipolar battery, which allows multiple electrodes to be connected in series without an external connector, simplifies the configuration of battery packs for electric vehicles such as 800V EVs, and thus improves the effciency of the battery structure. Through the simple series structure of cells, not only does the space utilization of the battery pack increase, but the number of parts used is reduced and the manufacturing process is simplified. This can even lead to cost savings. The number of parts can be reduced to 1/5 of the existing level, which in turn contributes to reducing the weight of the vehicle and improving fuel effciency. In addition, the bipolar structure has a short electron transfer path and it takes place in the vertical direction only, which helps to reduce resistance and maintain uniform heat generation characteristics, simplifying the management of cell heat generation in the pack. In the case of the monopolar structure, the path for transferring electrons is long, and heat generation characteristics vary by cell part under excessively high levels of charge and discharge conditions. LG Energy Solution’s Goal to Develop Bipolar Batteries LG Energy Solution is accelerating its research on bipolar batteries using bipolar process technology to become a solid leader in the next-generation battery market. The LG Energy Solution Future Technology Center formalized research and development R&D to develop semi-solid and all-solid batteries with a bipolar structure in June 2024, and aims to secure technology for full-scale commercialization of next-generation batteries, not just for simple prototype production, and enable large-scale mass production in the future. ion battery that we know. The bipolar structure that is newly attracting attention is bipolar, in which the cathode and anode are located above and below one current collector, and the electrolyte of the cell stack is separated and stacked. In other words, it can be said that it is a structure that stacks materials that make up a bipolar cell in a single large case, and it is possible to implement a series connection structure by simply stacking multiple electrodes without a separate external connector. Through this method, the desired voltage can be generated depending on the number of electrodes stacked in a single case. However, there is a technical difficulty in that the electrolyte should not be shared within a single case and should only function between a pair of cathode and anode. In addition, a short circuit may occur if leakage occurs. Therefore, the technical key of the bipolar structure is which type of electrolyte to implement and through which process.
